question_text: The gravitational field strength on Mars is approximately 4 N/kg, compared to 10 N/kg on Earth. How does the weight of an object on Mars compare to its weight on Earth?

options.label: A
options.text: The weight on Mars is 2.5 times greater than on Earth

options.label: B
options.text: The weight on Mars is 0.4 times (or 40%) the weight on Earth

options.label: C
options.text: The weight on Mars is the same as on Earth

options.label: D
options.text: The weight on Mars is 6 times less than on Earth

explanation: Since gravitational field strength on Mars is 4 N/kg and on Earth is 10 N/kg, the ratio is 4/10 = 0.4. This means any object weighs 0.4 times (or 40%) as much on Mars as it does on Earth. The same mass experiences a weaker gravitational pull on Mars.
